intro: Cyanea mannii, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits such as wound healing, pain relief, and infection prevention. The bioactive constituents of Cyanea mannii include sesquiterpenes and phenolic compounds, including cyanein and mannin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, although its use is currently restricted due to conservation efforts.
